info.name: Zario — Admin Phase
info.description:
Complete test suite for the Admin module.

## Setup
1. Set `baseUrl` variable to your server (e.g. http://localhost:5000)
2. Run the **0. Seed / Login** folder first — it populates `superAdminToken`, `adminToken`, `ownerToken`, and stores IDs you need for the other tests.
3. Run folders in order: Superadmin → Admin → Privilege Guards.

## Variables used
- `baseUrl`         — server root
- `superAdminToken` — access token from superadmin login
- `adminToken`      — access token from admin login
- `ownerToken`      — access token from owner login
- `createdAdminId`  — ID of the admin created by superadmin
- `createdAdminEmail`
- `targetUserId`    — owner user ID used in user-management tests
- `targetBusinessId`— business ID used in business-management tests

item.name: 0. Setup — Login as All Roles
item.description: Run these first. They store tokens and IDs into collection variables for all subsequent tests.

request.description:
Replace the email/password with what you used in the seed script.
Run `node scripts/seedSuperAdmin.js` first if you haven't already.

exec: console.log('✅ ownerToken and targetUserId stored:', body.data.user.id);

request.description:
Use an existing registered + email-verified owner account.
This sets targetUserId — used in all user management tests.

item.name: 1. Superadmin — Admin Management
item.description: Only superadmin can create and list admin accounts.

request.description: Admins skip email verification (isEmailVerified: true on creation), so login works immediately.

exec: pm.test('Status 403 — admin cannot list admins', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));
pm.test('Status 200 — superadmin can also access stats', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));
pm.test('Status 403 — owners cannot access admin stats', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));

item.name: 3. Admin — User Management
item.description: Requires targetUserId to be set from folder 0.

exec: pm.test('No superadmin accounts in admin view', () => {
pm.test('Status 403 — admin cannot filter by superadmin role', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));
// business may be null if user has none
pm.test('Status 422 — invalid ObjectId fails validation', () => pm.response.to.have.status(422));

request.description: Force-verifies email. Will return 409 if already verified — that's correct behaviour.

exec: pm.test('Status 409 — already verified returns conflict', () => pm.response.to.have.status(409));
// In development mode, _devResetToken should be present

request.description: Generates a password reset token and (in dev mode) returns it in _devResetToken.

exec: pm.test('Status 200 — role restored to owner', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

item.name: 3.14 Change User Role — Admin Tries to Assign Admin Role ✅ 403

exec: pm.test('Status 403 — admin cannot elevate to admin role', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));
pm.test('Status 403 — deactivated users are blocked at login', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));

request.description: After deactivation, the owner should be blocked from logging in.

item.name: 4. Admin — Business Management
item.description: Requires targetBusinessId. It gets set automatically by test 3.6 if the owner has a business. Otherwise set it manually.

exec: pm.test('Returns paginated businesses with owner populated', () => {
// Store the first business ID if targetBusinessId not yet set
pm.test('Status 422 — invalid plan name rejected', () => pm.response.to.have.status(422));

item.name: 4.8 Force Advance Onboarding to Step 5 (marks complete) ✅ 200
5. Privilege Guards
item.description: Confirm the role hierarchy is airtight.

exec: pm.test('Status 403 — delete is superadmin only', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));
// This requires knowing the superadmin's own userId.
// It will return 403 only if targetUserId === superadmin's ID.
// If not, it will delete the target user — set targetUserId to superadmin's ID first.

request.description:
⚠️ Before running: manually set targetUserId to the superadmin's own ID (from login response).
If targetUserId is NOT the superadmin ID, this will permanently delete that user.

exec: pm.test('Status 403 — admin cannot deactivate another admin', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));

request.description: Uses createdAdminId (another admin). Admin cannot touch a same-rank account.

item.name: 5.4 Owner Cannot Access Any Admin Route ✅ 403

exec: pm.test('Status 403 — owners have no access to admin routes', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));

item.name: 5.5 No Token — All Admin Routes Return 401 ✅ 401
6. Superadmin — Delete User (run last)
item.description: Destructive test — run last. Deletes the admin created in folder 1.

request.description: Uses createdAdminId set in test 1.1. This permanently deletes the test admin account.
